turns read the staged asset(s) as ordinary inputs.

contextStrategy.description:
Context-window management strategy. Defaults to `None` (no
compaction). Inert in the degenerate path.

deploymentModel.description:
Where/how each inference turn is dispatched — same field, defaults
and semantics as `AutomatedStep::deployment_model`. On the
degenerate single-shot path it reaches the full
`Executor{capacity}` / `Scheduled{lease}` dispatch in
`lower_automated_step`. The multi-turn loop path supports
`Executor { capacity: None }` only in v1 and compile-rejects the rest
(mirrors the `context_strategy` gate); per-turn pooled/scheduled
admission is a follow-up (docs/12).

images.description:
Vision inputs attached to the user message — each `{"path":
"{{<slug>.<field>}}", "media_type"?: "..."}`. Opaque JSON in the
model layer (same as `response_format`); the executor LLM backend
validates it and the compiler's LLM `ref_scanner` walks
`images[i].path` for `{{<slug>.<field>}}` borrows exactly as it
does for a single-shot LLM step. Empty by default. Carries the
vision capability that lets the Agent fully subsume the retired
LLM step.

maxTurns.description:
Hard cap on agent turns. `1` (default) is the single-shot LLM
call indistinguishable from `AutomatedStep(Llm)` — the degenerate
path the equivalence test pins down.

model.description:
LLM model + provider selection. Same shape the existing
`LlmConfig` carries in `executionSpec.config`; the degenerate
path uses these fields verbatim when constructing the equivalent
`LlmConfig` payload.

onToolError.description:
What happens when a tool call fails. Defaults to `Feedback`.
Inert in PR 1 (no tools).

responseFormat.description:
Optional response-format constraint (`{"type": "text"}` or
`{"type": "json_schema", "schema": {...}}`). Opaque JSON in the
model layer — the executor LLM backend validates it.

retryPolicy.description:
Retry behaviour on a per-turn inference failure/timeout. Same shape
and defaults as `AutomatedStep::retry_policy`. On the degenerate
(single-shot) path this threads straight through to the synthesized
`AutomatedStep(Llm)`. On the multi-turn loop path it caps the
executor's per-turn `max_retries`.

stopWhen.description:
Optional terminal Rhai guard. When `Some`, the agent loop exits
once this expression evaluates true on the parked agent state.
Inert in the degenerate (single-turn) path.

systemPrompt.description:
Optional system prompt template (supports `{{<slug>.<field>}}`
placeholders, same as the LLM `system_prompt` config field).

userPrompt.description:
Initial user prompt template (supports `{{<slug>.<field>}}`
placeholders, corresponds to `LlmConfig::prompt`).

oneOf.description:
Calls another published template as a child net and returns its
terminal result, correlated per invocation. Compiles (via
`Context::spawn`) to: a parent-side request/spawn effect, a
`bridge_out` carrying the mapped initial token to a freshly spawned
child instance, and `bridge_in` reply/failure places joined back on a
synthesized correlation key. The child template is resolved and its
compiled AIR is embedded at the *parent's* publish time (see
`version_pin`) so a later change to the child does not alter
already-published parents.

inputContract.description:
Display-only snapshot of the child's **input** contract — its
`Start { initial }` port. Reconciled at publish from the resolved
child and refreshed by the editor's `/io-contract` fetch, exactly
like `output`. The compiler re-derives the real child input from the
frozen child, so this field never feeds compilation: it exists so the
canvas can show "what this sub-workflow consumes" (the way a Start
node shows its declared fields) without opening the property panel.
Empty `in` port ⇒ not yet resolved / child declares no Start fields.

inputMapping.description:
Parent upstream token → child Start `initial` port fields. Each
entry's `expression` is a Rhai expression over the inbound token;
together they assemble the token bridged into the child. Empty
(the default) passes the inbound token through unchanged.

output.description:
Declared shape of the child's terminal result, mapped back onto the
workflow token at the join. Empty fields ⇒ pass the child result
through unchanged (Json). Authoring can prefill this from the
child's End `terminal` port.

templateId.description:
Stable identity of the child template family (any version row's
`base_template_id`/`id` — resolution picks the concrete row per
`version_pin` at publish time).

versionPin.description:
Which concrete version of the child to embed. `Latest` resolves the
family's `is_latest` row *at the parent's publish time* and freezes
that concrete version into the embedded AIR; `Pinned` freezes an
explicit version. Either way the published parent is deterministic.

oneOf.description:
Workflow-as-streaming-endpoint INGRESS (docs/25 §9 Phase 3). Declares
`Out` [`Channel`]s an external producer feeds through a mekhan ingress
endpoint; each declared channel surfaces as a named source handle the
graph wires downstream consumers off, exactly like an
[`WorkflowNodeData::AutomatedStep`]'s `Out` channels. A StreamSource
has NO control-flow handles in v1 — no inbound control edge, no
default `out`; its only handles are its channel handles. Lowering
synthesizes the standard per-channel place `p_{id}_{name}` (WI-2).

channels.description:
Declared streaming [`Channel`]s. Direction is expected to be `Out`
for every entry (the node *produces* into the net); enforced by
validation (WI-2), not the type. `#[serde(default)]` ⇒ existing
templates (field absent → empty) round-trip unchanged.

oneOf.description:
Workflow-as-streaming-endpoint EGRESS (docs/25 §9 Phase 3). Declares
exactly ONE `In` [`Channel`] (enforced by validation in WI-2, not the
type — the field stays `Vec<Channel>` so the three channel-bearing
variants share one accessor shape, see
[`WorkflowNodeData::channels`]). The upstream producer edge wires to
the channel's named target handle; mekhan exposes the sunk stream on
an egress endpoint. No control-flow handles in v1.

channels.description:
Declared streaming [`Channel`]s — exactly one `In` entry in v1
(validation-enforced, WI-2). `#[serde(default)]` ⇒ absent → empty.

owner_template_id.description:
Owning parent family base id (`COALESCE(base_template_id, id)`), set
only when `visibility == "private"`. A private sub-workflow may be
embedded only by this family and never runs standalone.

WorkspaceMember.description:
A single `workspace_members` row. `user_id` is derived from the OIDC
subject via `uuid_v5(SUBJECT_UUID_NAMESPACE, subject)` — the same
derivation the resolver uses.

display_name.description:
Human-readable identity, LEFT JOINed from `user_profiles` (populated by
the auth extractor on each authenticated request). `None` for a member
who was added by `subject` but has never logged into mekhan.

WorkspaceSummary.description:
Summary view returned by `GET /workspaces` and embedded in
`WorkspaceMember` responses.

tags.name: templates
tags.description: Workflow template CRUD, versioning, publish, compile-to-AIR.

tags.name: instances
tags.description: Running workflow instances deployed to the petri-lab engine.

tags.name: executions
tags.description: AutomatedStep execution introspection — data-plane channel byte taps (out-of-band streaming channel payloads over JetStream).

tags.name: processes
tags.description: HPI process inspection — metrics, logs, tasks, artifacts.

tags.name: processes-live
tags.description: SSE backfill + live streams for process metrics, logs, and artifacts.

tags.name: tasks
tags.description: Human task lifecycle — list, complete, cancel.

tags.name: catalogue
tags.description: Artifact catalogue, lineage, distinct-value filters.

tags.name: inventory
tags.description: Legacy file migration (docs/32) — by-reference physical-copy registry (`file_inventory`), content-addressed to the catalogue via `content_hash`. Batched register (no bytes) + list/stats.

tags.name: file_servers
tags.description: First-class storage backends (docs/32 §4.1) — the entities the platform tracks files on. Identity-only parent + N access-method endpoints (object_store|s3|sftp|local_mount); derived rollups here; connection + secrets in the referenced workspace `resource`.

tags.name: data
tags.description: Unified Data browser read-model — catalogued logical entries with their physical copies (inventory joined by `content_hash`, file-server names resolved) plus uncatalogued (index-only) files. Consolidates the catalogue + inventory views.

tags.name: provenance
tags.description: Token ancestry walks and cross-net signal links.

tags.name: files
tags.description: Per-template file upload/download (50 MB limit, S3-backed).

tags.name: triggers
tags.description: Workflow triggers — cron/catalog/lifecycle/webhook/manual entry points.

tags.name: auth-tokens
tags.description: Embedded per-user automation tokens (Zitadel-backed PATs).

tags.name: resources
tags.description: Typed credential CRUD (`postgres`, `openai`, `s3`, `slack`, `google_oauth`). Workflows bind aliases to resources at launch; secrets live in Vault.

tags.name: assets
tags.description: User-typed, curated static content (docs/20). Asset types are user-defined `PortField` schemas; assets are version-pinned, scope-owned collections of schema-validated JSONB records (+ S3 for File fields), consumed by nodes as staged inputs.

tags.name: job-templates
tags.description: Versioned cluster job-spec entity (flavor-tagged `slurm`/`nomad`) — typed common spec + flavor escape hatch + declared parameters, staged onto datacenter resources. No secret coupling.

tags.name: container-images
tags.description: Container image materialization onto datacenter clusters.

tags.name: backends
tags.description: AutomatedStep backend registry — display metadata, default config, default output port, dispatch mode.

tags.name: node-types
tags.description: Workflow node-type registry — per-variant display metadata, runtime kind, and protocol flags.

tags.name: health
tags.description: Liveness probe.

tags.name: workspaces
tags.description: Tenant boundaries — membership + member admin (Phase A2).

tags.name: folders
tags.description: Workspace-scoped hierarchical template grouping (single-parent tree) + tag/visibility surface + per-folder OpenAPI bundle.

tags.name: me
tags.description: Per-session preferences — active workspace switcher.

tags.name: users
tags.description: Directory lookups — email → OIDC subject resolver for member admin.

tags.name: admin
tags.description: Operator-only maintenance — remove / reseed the built-in demo workflows.

tags.name: clusters
tags.description: Multi-cluster scheduling control plane — live datacenter cluster clients (connection health, watcher state, active leases) + force-reconnect / drain (read-through of the engine `ClusterRegistry`).

tags.name: runners
tags.description: Lab Runner Fleet — workspace-scoped runners + GitLab-style enrollment. Public `POST /enroll` is authed by a `rt_` registration token in the body; runners then authenticate with a mekhan-native `rnr_` bearer (SHA-256 hash stored, works offline).

tags.name: roster
tags.description: Humans as a capacity (docs/33 §7) — the set of `workspace_members` enrolled into a human `capacity` resource backed by a `pool-<capacity_id>` net (the human counterpart to the runner fleet). Admin management (enroll / update / revoke, caps validated against the capability registry) + self-service (`/roster/me`, `/roster/availability` durable toggle). A member's availability drives presence admission, reusing the runner pool plumbing verbatim.

tags.name: workers
tags.description: Grouped + Enrolled Workers — the identity plane on the executor worker pool: enrolled, group-scoped, revocable workers that still PULL. Public `POST /enroll` is authed by a `wt_` registration token in the body; workers then authenticate with a mekhan-native `wkr_` bearer. A group is backed by a `capacity` resource with the `worker` preset. Plus the anonymous worker-pool coverage read.

tags.name: capability-types
tags.description: Phase 4 — admin-curated, workspace-scoped typed capability registry. Runner-advertised capabilities (enroll) + step Requirements (publish) are typed against these. Create/revoke are cookie-only (browser admin boundary).

tags.name: capacities
tags.description: Capacity aggregator (docs/23 + docs/24) — the unified Control-Plane read: every `capacity` + `datacenter` resource classified by the SINGLE dispatch authority (`CapacityAxes::backend`) with live utilization (token holders / presence online / worker coverage / scheduler lease state).

tags.name: models
tags.description: Model-pool control plane (docs/28 + docs/29) — the loaded-set projection (operator-curated `model_states` AND-gated against live runner interface catalogs) that sources the editor model picker, plus the operator state-machine transition (`approved → loading → loaded → draining → unloaded`). Projection/control seam only: inference bypasses the engine net + presence net; no NATS subjects.
